pi_items.source: OpenBCI hardware specifications and product documentation.
OpenBCI hardware specifications confirming the Ganglion board natively has 4 EEG channels.
International 10-20 System for EEG electrode placement; mapping O1/O2 (Occipital) and P3/P4 (Parietal) to visual processing regions.
Anatomical brain mapping: the Occipital and Parietal lobes, which process visual information, are located in the posterior (back/bottom) region of the head.

privileged_info_summary: The OpenBCI Ganglion board explicitly supports exactly 4 channels. Standard 10-20 EEG electrode placement confirms that visual cortex activity is best captured by posterior electrodes: O1 and O2 (occipital lobe) for primary visual processing, along with P3 and P4 (parietal lobe) for visual-spatial processing. The data used for this task is natively generated by the OpenBCI GUI's synthetic algorithmic source.
